title: Walking Up A Hill At Dawn
content:
Here is the wind in the morning;
    The kind red face of God
    Is looking over the hill
    We are climbing.

    To-morrow we are going to marry
    And work and play together,
    And laugh together at things
    Which would not amuse our neighbours.

    Song of Kafiristan.
meaning:
"Walking Up A Hill At Dawn" is a quintessential example of Edward Powys Mathers (As Translator)'s signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
